content.content:
[{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/comments/3564690931","pull_request_review_id":4678432855,"id":3564690931,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAhFMk87UeN3z","diff_hunk":"@@ -149,8 +149,39 @@
   home.pointerCursor = {
     gtk.enable = true;
     # x11.enable = true;
+    # Hyprland 0.55 uses hyprcursor by default, but bibata-cursors ships only an
+    # XCursor theme, so Hyprland can't resolve Bibata and falls back to a generic
+    # default cursor. Enabling hyprcursor here exports HYPRCURSOR_THEME/SIZE so
+    # Hyprland looks for a hyprcursor theme named "Bibata-Modern-Ice" (this
+    # home-manager version only sets the env vars; the theme itself is generated
+    # from the XCursor theme in the xdg.dataFile entry below).
+    hyprcursor.enable = true;
     package = pkgs.bibata-cursors;
     name = "Bibata-Modern-Ice";
     size = 24;
   };
+
+  # bibata-cursors is XCursor-only, so convert it to hyprcursor format for
+  # Hyprland 0.55 (which defaults to hyprcursor). hyprcursor-util --extract
+  # decompiles the XCursor theme (needs xcur2png) and --create compiles a
+  # hyprcursor theme. The manifest name is set to "Bibata-Modern-Ice" so
+  # Hyprland resolves HYPRCURSOR_THEME to this theme (hyprcursor matches on the
+  # manifest name or the directory stem). Installed under ~/.local/share/icons,
+  # which libhyprcursor searches; the XCursor theme keeps its own dir untouched.
+  xdg.dataFile."icons/Bibata-Modern-Ice-hyprcursor".source =
+    pkgs.runCommand "bibata-modern-ice-hyprcursor"
+      {
+        nativeBuildInputs = [
+          pkgs.hyprcursor
+          pkgs.xcur2png
+        ];
+      }
+      ''
+        hyprcursor-util --extract ${pkgs.bibata-cursors}/share/icons/Bibata-Modern-Ice --output .
+        substituteInPlace extracted_Bibata-Modern-Ice/manifest.hl \
+          --replace-fail "name = Extracted Theme" "name = Bibata-Modern-Ice"
+        hyprcursor-util --create extracted_Bibata-Modern-Ice --output .
+        mkdir -p "$out"
+        cp -r "theme_Bibata-Modern-Ice/." "$out/"","path":"home/desktop.nix","commit_id":"73f9e98e90a51a80013320302bcb41a10f9682f7","original_commit_id":"73f9e98e90a51a80013320302bcb41a10f9682f7","user":{"login":"gemini-code-assist[bot]","id":176961590,"node_id":"BOT_kgDOCow4Ng","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/956858?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D","html_url":"https://github.com/apps/gemini-code-assist","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/received_events","type":"Bot","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

Instead of creating the directory first and copying its contents using the `/.` syntax, you can copy the directory directly to `$out`. Since `$out` does not exist yet, `cp -r` will create it and copy the contents of `theme_Bibata-Modern-Ice` directly into it. This is cleaner and more idiomatic in Nix derivations.

```
        cp -r theme_Bibata-Modern-Ice "$out"
```","created_at":"2026-07-11T17:24:32Z","updated_at":"2026-07-11T17:24:32Z","html_url":"https://github.com/patflynn/cosmo/pull/611#discussion_r3564690931","pull_request_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/611","_links":{"self":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/comments/3564690931"},"html":{"href":"https://github.com/patflynn/cosmo/pull/611#discussion_r3564690931"},"pull_request":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/611"}},"reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/comments/3564690931/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"start_line":184,"original_start_line":184,"start_side":"RIGHT","line":185,"original_line":185,"side":"RIGHT","author_association":"NONE","original_position":37,"position":37,"subject_type":"line"}]

content.content:
{"body":"## Problem

After a reboot activated a pending system upgrade, the Hyprland cursor on
`classic-laddie` (and any host using this home profile) changed to a generic,
default-looking cursor instead of the configured Bibata.

## Root cause (verified on the live system)

- The cursor is configured in `home/desktop.nix` via `home.pointerCursor` as
  `Bibata-Modern-Ice` (`pkgs.bibata-cursors`), but only as an **XCursor** theme
  (`gtk.enable = true`; no hyprcursor). The `bibata-cursors` package ships
  XCursor format only — `cursors/` + `index.theme`, no `manifest.hl`.
- Hyprland 0.55.4 (current on the host) defaults to **hyprcursor**
  (`cursor:enable_hyprcursor = 1`, confirmed via `hyprctl`). With no hyprcursor
  theme available, Hyprland cannot resolve Bibata and falls back to its built-in
  default cursor — exactly the symptom the upgrade surfaced.

## Fix

`home/desktop.nix`, `home.pointerCursor` block only, plus one generated theme:

1. `home.pointerCursor.hyprcursor.enable = true;` — exports
   `HYPRCURSOR_THEME=Bibata-Modern-Ice` / `HYPRCURSOR_SIZE=24`.
2. A generated hyprcursor theme installed under
   `~/.local/share/icons/Bibata-Modern-Ice-hyprcursor`, built by converting
   Bibata's XCursor theme with `hyprcursor-util` (`--extract` via `xcur2png`,
   then `--create`), with the manifest `name` set to `Bibata-Modern-Ice`.

### Why the extra step (important)

The pinned home-manager in this flake (`abfad3d`, release 26.11, locked
2025-04-24) implements `home.pointerCursor.hyprcursor.enable` as **env vars
only** — it sets `HYPRCURSOR_THEME`/`HYPRCURSOR_SIZE` but does **not** run
`hyprcursor-util` or generate any hyprcursor theme (see
`modules/config/home-cursor.nix`). So the option alone does not fix the
xcursor-only Bibata. This PR therefore also generates the actual hyprcursor
theme, as the task's fallback path anticipated.

hyprcursor resolves a theme when either the `manifest.hl` `name` **or** the
directory stem matches the requested name (`libhyprcursor/hyprcursor.cpp`:
`if (NAME != name && name != themeDir.path().stem().string()) continue;`), and
only considers dirs that contain a `manifest.hl`. Setting the manifest name to
`Bibata-Modern-Ice` makes Hyprland pick this theme, while the original
XCursor `icons/Bibata-Modern-Ice` dir (no manifest) is ignored by hyprcursor
and left untouched for GTK/X apps.

## Verification

- `nix eval …home.pointerCursor.hyprcursor.enable` → `true`.
- `home.sessionVariables` → `HYPRCURSOR_THEME=Bibata-Modern-Ice`,
  `HYPRCURSOR_SIZE=24`.
- Built the generated theme derivation directly and inspected the output:
  `manifest.hl` with `name = Bibata-Modern-Ice` and `hyprcursors/` containing
  **56 compiled `.hlc` shapes**.
- `xdg.dataFile."icons/Bibata-Modern-Ice-hyprcursor".target` →
  `.local/share/icons/Bibata-Modern-Ice-hyprcursor` (a dir libhyprcursor
  searches).
- `nix build --dry-run` of
  `.#nixosConfigurations.classic-laddie.config.system.build.toplevel`
  evaluates cleanly (no eval errors introduced).
- `nixfmt` / `treefmt`: 0 changes; pre-commit hooks and `klaus _pre-review`
  pass with no findings.

### Note on the full toplevel build

`nix build …toplevel` currently fails on an **unrelated, pre-existing** issue:
`catppuccin-gtk` → `python3.14-catppuccin-2.5.0` breaks because
`matplotlib.style` has no `core` attribute. This reproduces identically on the
unmodified tree (same derivation hash), so it is not caused by this change and
is orthogonal to the cursor fix.

## After merge

Rebuild, then re-login (or run `hyprctl setcursor Bibata-Modern-Ice 24`) for
the cursor to take effect.

Run: 20260711-1313-b7ae7d79
","files":[{"path":"home/desktop.nix","additions":31,"deletions":0,"changeType":"MODIFIED"}],"headRefName":"agent/20260711-1313-b7ae7d79","title":"home/desktop: fix Hyprland default-cursor fallback by generating a hyprcursor Bibata theme"}

{"body":"## Problem

After a reboot activated a pending system upgrade, the Hyprland cursor on
`classic-laddie` (and any host using this home profile) changed to a generic,
default-looking cursor instead of the configured Bibata.

## Root cause (verified on the live system)

- The cursor is configured in `home/desktop.nix` via `home.pointerCursor` as
  `Bibata-Modern-Ice` (`pkgs.bibata-cursors`), but only as an **XCursor** theme
  (`gtk.enable = true`; no hyprcursor). The `bibata-cursors` package ships
  XCursor format only — `cursors/` + `index.theme`, no `manifest.hl`.
- Hyprland 0.55.4 (current on the host) defaults to **hyprcursor**
  (`cursor:enable_hyprcursor = 1`, confirmed via `hyprctl`). With no hyprcursor
  theme available, Hyprland cannot resolve Bibata and falls back to its built-in
  default cursor — exactly the symptom the upgrade surfaced.

## Fix

`home/desktop.nix`, `home.pointerCursor` block only, plus one generated theme:

1. `home.pointerCursor.hyprcursor.enable = true;` — exports
   `HYPRCURSOR_THEME=Bibata-Modern-Ice` / `HYPRCURSOR_SIZE=24`.
2. A generated hyprcursor theme installed under
   `~/.local/share/icons/Bibata-Modern-Ice-hyprcursor`, built by converting
   Bibata's XCursor theme with `hyprcursor-util` (`--extract` via `xcur2png`,
   then `--create`), with the manifest `name` set to `Bibata-Modern-Ice`.

### Why the extra step (important)

The pinned home-manager in this flake (`abfad3d`, release 26.11, locked
2025-04-24) implements `home.pointerCursor.hyprcursor.enable` as **env vars
only** — it sets `HYPRCURSOR_THEME`/`HYPRCURSOR_SIZE` but does **not** run
`hyprcursor-util` or generate any hyprcursor theme (see
`modules/config/home-cursor.nix`). So the option alone does not fix the
xcursor-only Bibata. This PR therefore also generates the actual hyprcursor
theme, as the task's fallback path anticipated.

hyprcursor resolves a theme when either the `manifest.hl` `name` **or** the
directory stem matches the requested name (`libhyprcursor/hyprcursor.cpp`:
`if (NAME != name && name != themeDir.path().stem().string()) continue;`), and
only considers dirs that contain a `manifest.hl`. Setting the manifest name to
`Bibata-Modern-Ice` makes Hyprland pick this theme, while the original
XCursor `icons/Bibata-Modern-Ice` dir (no manifest) is ignored by hyprcursor
and left untouched for GTK/X apps.

## Verification

- `nix eval …home.pointerCursor.hyprcursor.enable` → `true`.
- `home.sessionVariables` → `HYPRCURSOR_THEME=Bibata-Modern-Ice`,
  `HYPRCURSOR_SIZE=24`.
- Built the generated theme derivation directly and inspected the output:
  `manifest.hl` with `name = Bibata-Modern-Ice` and `hyprcursors/` containing
  **56 compiled `.hlc` shapes**.
- `xdg.dataFile."icons/Bibata-Modern-Ice-hyprcursor".target` →
  `.local/share/icons/Bibata-Modern-Ice-hyprcursor` (a dir libhyprcursor
  searches).
- `nix build --dry-run` of
  `.#nixosConfigurations.classic-laddie.config.system.build.toplevel`
  evaluates cleanly (no eval errors introduced).
- `nixfmt` / `treefmt`: 0 changes; pre-commit hooks and `klaus _pre-review`
  pass with no findings.

### Note on the full toplevel build

`nix build …toplevel` currently fails on an **unrelated, pre-existing** issue:
`catppuccin-gtk` → `python3.14-catppuccin-2.5.0` breaks because
`matplotlib.style` has no `core` attribute. This reproduces identically on the
unmodified tree (same derivation hash), so it is not caused by this change and
is orthogonal to the cursor fix.

## After merge

Rebuild, then re-login (or run `hyprctl setcursor Bibata-Modern-Ice 24`) for
the cursor to take effect.

Run: 20260711-1313-b7ae7d79

content.text: The comment is a reasonable idiomatic cleanup. Let me apply it.

content.text: Parse is clean. The change is a straightforward, well-understood `cp` behavior swap. Let me commit and push.

input.command: git add -A && git commit -q -m "home/desktop: simplify hyprcursor theme copy in derivation" && git push 2>&1 | tail -5

content.text: Pushed (nixfmt pre-commit hook passed). Now replying to the review comment.

input.command: gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/611/comments/3564690931/replies -f body='Done — replaced the `mkdir -p "$out"` + `cp -r "theme_Bibata-Modern-Ice/." "$out/"` with `cp -r theme_Bibata-Modern-Ice "$out"`, which creates `$out` and copies the theme contents into it in one step.'

content.text:
Done. There was one review comment on PR #611 (from gemini-code-assist), and I addressed it.

**Change:** In `home/desktop.nix`, simplified the hyprcursor theme derivation's final copy step per the reviewer's suggestion — replaced `mkdir -p "$out"` + `cp -r "theme_Bibata-Modern-Ice/." "$out/"` with the more idiomatic `cp -r theme_Bibata-Modern-Ice "$out"`. Since `$out` doesn't exist yet, `cp -r` creates it and copies the theme contents into it in one step.

**Verified:** `nix-instantiate --parse` passes; nixfmt pre-commit hook passed on push. Committed (`33cff47`), pushed, and replied to the review comment.
